Wojciech Szczęsny.bio: World-class goalkeeper who came out of retirement to join Barcelona after a stellar career at Juventus and Arsenal. One of the most experienced goalkeepers in international football with 90 caps.
Wojciech Szczęsny.playingStyle: Commanding goalkeeper with exceptional shot-stopping reflexes, brave in one-on-one situations, and a confident presence who organizes his defense vocally.
Wojciech Szczęsny.worldCup2026Role: Starting goalkeeper and one of the most senior figures in the squad, providing big-game experience from the back.
Wojciech Szczęsny.notableFact: Came out of international retirement and joined Barcelona in a remarkable late-career move, becoming Barca's first-choice keeper.

Matty Cash.bio: English-born right-back who chose to represent Poland through his Polish heritage. A Premier League regular at Aston Villa known for his attacking energy and defensive reliability.
Matty Cash.playingStyle: Modern attacking fullback who excels at overlapping runs, delivering crosses, and contributing to the attack while maintaining solid defensive positioning.
Matty Cash.worldCup2026Role: Starting right-back providing Premier League quality and attacking width on the right flank.
Matty Cash.notableFact: Born and raised in England, he switched international allegiance to Poland through his Polish ancestry, quickly becoming a key player.

Jakub Kiwior.bio: Left-footed center-back who developed at Spezia in Italy before joining Arsenal and later moving to FC Porto. A composed defender comfortable in possession-based systems.
Jakub Kiwior.playingStyle: Ball-playing center-back with excellent left foot, comfortable stepping into midfield with the ball and distributing accurately under pressure.
Jakub Kiwior.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back whose ability to play out from the back is crucial to Poland's build-up play.
Jakub Kiwior.notableFact: His move from Spezia to Arsenal was one of the biggest transfers for a Polish defender, showcasing his rapid development in Italian football.

Sebastian Walukiewicz.bio: Young center-back who has developed steadily in Serie A with Torino after coming through the Gornik Zabrze academy in Poland. A physical and improving defender.
Sebastian Walukiewicz.playingStyle: Strong and aggressive center-back who is dominant in aerial and physical duels, with improving distribution skills.
Sebastian Walukiewicz.worldCup2026Role: Center-back depth option who can step in to provide physical defensive presence when needed.
Sebastian Walukiewicz.notableFact: Moved to Serie A as a teenager, showing the courage to test himself in one of the world's most tactically demanding leagues at a young age.

Jan Bednarek.bio: Experienced center-back who has been a reliable performer in the Premier League with Southampton. A consistent figure in the Polish national team for nearly a decade.
Jan Bednarek.playingStyle: Solid, no-frills center-back who excels in reading the game, positioning, and making timely interceptions and blocks.
Jan Bednarek.worldCup2026Role: Experienced center-back option competing for a starting spot, bringing Premier League experience and reliability.
Jan Bednarek.notableFact: Has been one of Southampton's most consistent performers across multiple seasons, earning respect in the Premier League for his dedication.

Jakub Moder.bio: Dynamic midfielder who joined Brighton from Lech Poznan and has shown flashes of brilliance in the Premier League. Overcame a serious knee injury to return as a key player.
Jakub Moder.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder with excellent technical ability, capable of scoring spectacular goals and driving forward from deep positions.
Jakub Moder.worldCup2026Role: Starting central midfielder whose energy and technical ability are central to Poland's midfield engine.
Jakub Moder.notableFact: Made a remarkable comeback from a serious ACL injury, demonstrating incredible mental strength to return to top-level Premier League football.

Kamil Grosicki.bio: Veteran winger with blistering pace who has been a fan favorite for Poland across multiple major tournaments. Now back in the Ekstraklasa with Pogon Szczecin at the twilight of his career.
Kamil Grosicki.playingStyle: Lightning-fast winger who stretches defenses with his pace, direct dribbling, and ability to deliver crosses at speed.
Kamil Grosicki.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player who can provide a spark off the bench with his pace, particularly in games where Poland need to stretch tired defenses.
Kamil Grosicki.notableFact: Has represented Poland at multiple World Cups and European Championships, becoming one of the most capped Polish wingers of the modern era with 95 caps.

Piotr Zieliński.bio: Poland's most technically gifted midfielder who made a marquee move to Inter Milan after years of excellence at Napoli, where he won the Serie A title. A key creative force in the squad.
Piotr Zieliński.playingStyle: Elegant playmaker who controls the game with his passing range, vision, and ability to score goals from midfield with both feet.
Piotr Zieliński.worldCup2026Role: Key creative midfielder and one of Poland's most important players, the primary source of chances from central areas.
Piotr Zieliński.notableFact: Was a central part of Napoli's historic 2022-23 Serie A title win, their first Scudetto in over 30 years, before earning a move to Inter Milan.

Robert Lewandowski.bio: One of the greatest strikers in football history, holder of numerous goalscoring records including 84 goals for Poland. The Barcelona forward is his country's all-time leading scorer and the face of Polish football worldwide.
Robert Lewandowski.playingStyle: Complete striker with exceptional finishing, positioning, heading, and the ability to score from any angle with either foot. Combines predatory instincts with intelligent build-up play.
Robert Lewandowski.worldCup2026Role: Captain, talisman, and starting striker. Everything runs through Lewandowski, who will likely be playing his final World Cup.
Robert Lewandowski.notableFact: Holds the record for most goals in a single Bundesliga season with 41, breaking Gerd Muller's 49-year-old record, and has scored over 600 career goals.

Sebastian Szymański.bio: Creative attacking midfielder who has been brilliant at Fenerbahce in Turkey after developing at Feyenoord. One of Poland's most in-form players in recent years.
Sebastian Szymański.playingStyle: Technically gifted playmaker who excels in the final third with his vision, set-piece delivery, and ability to unlock defenses with incisive passes.
Sebastian Szymański.worldCup2026Role: Starting attacking midfielder or second striker, providing the creative link between midfield and Lewandowski.
Sebastian Szymański.notableFact: Has been one of the standout performers in Turkish football, earning rave reviews for his creative output at Fenerbahce.

Przemysław Frankowski.bio: Tireless right winger who has found his best form at Stade Rennais in Ligue 1 after a stint in MLS with Chicago Fire. A reliable performer who contributes on both sides of the ball.
Przemysław Frankowski.playingStyle: Hardworking winger who combines attacking threat with defensive diligence, providing width and quality crossing from the right side.
Przemysław Frankowski.worldCup2026Role: Starting right winger whose two-way play and consistency make him a reliable choice in Poland's attack.
Przemysław Frankowski.notableFact: Successfully transitioned from MLS to Ligue 1, proving doubters wrong and establishing himself as one of the best Polish players in French football.

Łukasz Skorupski.bio: Experienced goalkeeper who has been a consistent performer in Serie A with Bologna for many years. A reliable second-choice behind Szczesny in the national team.
Łukasz Skorupski.playingStyle: Well-rounded goalkeeper with solid shot-stopping and good distribution, comfortable playing out from the back in Bologna's system.
Łukasz Skorupski.worldCup2026Role: Quality backup goalkeeper who could step in seamlessly if Szczesny is unavailable.
Łukasz Skorupski.notableFact: Has been a loyal servant to Bologna for multiple seasons, helping them achieve their best Serie A finishes in years and qualify for European competition.

Bartosz Bereszyński.bio: Veteran right-back who has spent years in Italian football, most recently at Serie B with Palermo after a long spell at Sampdoria. A dependable defensive option.
Bartosz Bereszyński.playingStyle: Defensively disciplined fullback who prioritizes solidity over attacking flair, effective in one-on-one situations.
Bartosz Bereszyński.worldCup2026Role: Experienced backup right-back providing depth and Italian league experience.
Bartosz Bereszyński.notableFact: Spent many years at Sampdoria in Serie A, becoming one of the most consistent Polish defenders in Italian football.

Taras Romanczuk.bio: Veteran defensive midfielder and captain of Jagiellonia Bialystok, who led them to a historic Polish league title. A late bloomer in the national team.
Taras Romanczuk.playingStyle: Disciplined holding midfielder who screens the defense effectively, winning the ball back with intelligent positioning rather than reckless challenges.
Taras Romanczuk.worldCup2026Role: Experienced squad player who can provide defensive midfield cover and adds veteran presence.
Taras Romanczuk.notableFact: Was instrumental in Jagiellonia Bialystok's historic first-ever Ekstraklasa title, one of the biggest underdog stories in Polish football.

Kamil Glik.bio: Legendary Polish center-back with 100 caps who had a distinguished career at Torino and Monaco. Now back in Poland with Cracovia, he brings unmatched international experience.
Kamil Glik.playingStyle: Old-school warrior center-back who is dominant in aerial duels, never backs down from a physical challenge, and leads by example.
Kamil Glik.worldCup2026Role: Senior squad mentor and emergency defensive option, bringing invaluable experience and dressing room leadership.
Kamil Glik.notableFact: Reached 100 caps for Poland, one of the few defenders to reach the milestone, and was a key figure in Monaco's stunning 2016-17 Ligue 1 title win.

Karol Świderski.bio: Striker who has played in Greece, MLS, and the Greek Super League, currently at Panathinaikos. A versatile forward who offers an alternative to Lewandowski.
Karol Świderski.playingStyle: Mobile striker who works hard for the team, capable of playing across the front line and contributing with goals and assists.
Karol Świderski.worldCup2026Role: Backup striker and impact substitute who provides a different option to Lewandowski from the bench.
Karol Świderski.notableFact: Had a successful stint at Charlotte FC in MLS before returning to European football, showing his adaptability across different leagues.

Nicola Zalewski.bio: Roma academy product born in Italy to Polish parents, who has become one of the most exciting wing-backs in Serie A. A modern full-back with attacking flair and pace.
Nicola Zalewski.playingStyle: Dynamic wing-back who surges forward with pace and skill, comfortable in one-on-one situations and capable of creating chances with his dribbling.
Nicola Zalewski.worldCup2026Role: Starting left-back whose pace and attacking quality provide a crucial outlet on the left side.
Nicola Zalewski.notableFact: Born and raised in Rome, he came through Roma's academy and chose to represent Poland internationally, becoming a key player for both club and country.

Bartosz Slisz.bio: Energetic midfielder who moved to MLS with Atlanta United after impressing at Legia Warsaw. A tireless runner who covers enormous ground in midfield.
Bartosz Slisz.playingStyle: High-energy box-to-box midfielder who covers every blade of grass, effective in both defensive and attacking phases with his relentless running.
Bartosz Slisz.worldCup2026Role: Midfield rotation option who provides energy and defensive solidity, particularly useful when Poland need to control possession.
Bartosz Slisz.notableFact: Was one of the most coveted midfielders in the Ekstraklasa before his move to MLS, where he has continued to impress with Atlanta United.

Adam Buksa.bio: Tall, physical striker who has played in MLS and European football, currently at Midtjylland in Denmark. A classic target man who is a major aerial threat.
Adam Buksa.playingStyle: Towering target striker who wins aerial duels, holds the ball up effectively, and provides a focal point for the attack.
Adam Buksa.worldCup2026Role: Target man option off the bench, especially useful for set pieces and when Poland need a direct Plan B approach.
Adam Buksa.notableFact: Standing at 193cm, his aerial ability makes him one of the most dangerous set-piece threats in the entire squad.

Kacper Urbański.bio: Teenage prodigy who has broken into Bologna's first team in Serie A at a remarkably young age. One of the most exciting young talents in Polish football.
Kacper Urbański.playingStyle: Technically gifted young playmaker with silky close control, mature passing ability, and composure well beyond his years.
Kacper Urbański.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young talent who represents Poland's future, capable of providing creative magic as a substitute.
Kacper Urbański.notableFact: At just 21, he is one of the youngest players in the squad and has already made his mark in Serie A, drawing comparisons to Poland's best ever midfielders.

Kamil Piątkowski.bio: Young center-back who has developed well at Red Bull Salzburg in the Austrian Bundesliga. Part of the Red Bull development pathway that has produced numerous top defenders.
Kamil Piątkowski.playingStyle: Athletic and aggressive center-back with good recovery pace, comfortable defending in a high line and pushing up to win the ball.
Kamil Piątkowski.worldCup2026Role: Young center-back option who can step in when needed, providing athleticism and energy in the backline.
Kamil Piątkowski.notableFact: Came through the Red Bull Salzburg system, which has produced some of Europe's best young defenders in recent years.

Marcin Bułka.bio: Young goalkeeper with elite pedigree, having trained at PSG and Chelsea before establishing himself as the number one at Nice in Ligue 1. An imposing physical presence at 199cm.
Marcin Bułka.playingStyle: Commanding modern goalkeeper who dominates his area with his enormous frame, good with his feet, and agile despite his height.
Marcin Bułka.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper and future number one candidate, gaining valuable international tournament experience.
Marcin Bułka.notableFact: At 199cm, he is one of the tallest goalkeepers at the World Cup. He trained alongside some of the world's best at PSG and Chelsea before carving his own path.

Krzysztof Piątek.bio: Striker who burst onto the scene with a phenomenal scoring run at Genoa and AC Milan, earning the nickname 'Il Pistolero' for his trademark goal celebration. Currently playing in Turkey.
Krzysztof Piątek.playingStyle: Instinctive poacher who thrives on chances inside the box, lethal with his right foot and possesses a natural striker's instinct for goal.
Krzysztof Piątek.worldCup2026Role: Experienced striker providing goalscoring depth, capable of making an immediate impact off the bench.
Krzysztof Piątek.notableFact: His debut months at AC Milan were sensational, scoring 9 goals in his first 12 games, with his pistol celebration becoming iconic across European football.

Jakub Piotrowski.bio: Versatile midfielder playing in Bulgaria with Ludogorets, where he has been a key performer in their domestic dominance and European campaigns.
Jakub Piotrowski.playingStyle: Versatile midfielder who can play multiple positions in the center of the park, offering both creative and defensive contributions.
Jakub Piotrowski.worldCup2026Role: Utility midfielder providing squad depth across multiple central midfield positions.
Jakub Piotrowski.notableFact: Has been part of Ludogorets' remarkable run of consecutive Bulgarian league titles, gaining regular European experience.

Paweł Dawidowicz.bio: Center-back who has been a steady performer in Serie A with Hellas Verona for several seasons. Brings Italian defensive discipline to the national team setup.
Paweł Dawidowicz.playingStyle: Tactically astute defender who has been schooled in Italian defensive principles, excellent at reading danger and making timely interventions.
Paweł Dawidowicz.worldCup2026Role: Experienced Serie A center-back providing reliable defensive depth in the squad.
Paweł Dawidowicz.notableFact: Has spent nearly his entire professional career in Italian football, absorbing the Serie A defensive culture that has produced some of the world's best defenders.

Michał Skóraś.bio: Exciting young winger who has been impressing at Club Brugge in Belgium, one of the top clubs in the Belgian First Division. A tricky and creative attacker.
Michał Skóraś.playingStyle: Skillful winger with quick feet and creative instincts, capable of beating defenders with stepovers and changes of pace before delivering dangerous crosses or shots.
Michał Skóraś.worldCup2026Role: Young attacking option who can provide creativity and directness from the wing, especially as a second-half substitute.
Michał Skóraś.notableFact: Has been a standout performer at Club Brugge, helping them compete in the Champions League and establishing himself as one of Belgium's most exciting imports.
